name: Walking Street
description: Walking Street (Thai: ถนนคนเดิน) is an entertainment and red-light district in the city of Pattaya, Thailand. The street is a tourist attraction which draws both foreigners and Thai nationals primarily for its night life. The Walking Street area includes seafood restaurants, live music venues, beer bars, discothèques, sports bars, go-go bars, nightclubs and hotels. Also tourists are able to watch a variety of sex shows.In addition to a large number of institutions offering sexual entertainment, a significant number of prostitutes, both girls and gay men, work on the street. Although prostitution in Thailand is illegal, there are estimated to be 27,000 prostitutes working in Pattaya, many in Walking Street.Many of the prostitutes work as bargirls in the bars and clubs along the street. name: Hua Hin Walking Street
description: Hua Hin's red-light district and walking street full of go-go bars and freelancers.Soi Bintabaht stands out as Hua Hin's premier nightlife spot, capturing the attention of both tourists and expats. This lively area resides in the heart of downtown, a bustling narrow street adorned with compact bars, a few guesthouses, and recently, a restaurant. Don't fret about dining options though, as numerous eateries are within walking distance, catering to a wide array of culinary desires.After dinner, Soi Bintabaht becomes the go-to destination for an enjoyable evening. Over the past decade, this road has expanded, spawning several adjacent lanes that offer similar entertainment. Many expats simply refer to it as the Bintabaht area, acknowledging the overflow of amusement into the surrounding vicinity.Nearly all the bars here are tended to by ladies of the night, making this the primary Hua Hin red light district. Nonetheless, don't anticipate the wild a-go-go bars or anything remotely akin to what's found in Bangkok or other Thai cities.

description: Christin Massage is the exclusive body-to-body Thai massage services in Patong.Although there are a few similar massage parlors in Phuket town, they tend to cater more to locals, so tourists often opt for Christin Massage instead. The manager will ask if you’d like a drink and inquire about the type of massage you’re interested in. Meanwhile, you’ll be able to peruse the selection of girls dressed in attractive clothing and sporting numbers on their attire.Once you’ve made your choice, the manager will inform the girl, and the two of you will head upstairs to a private room. All of the rooms at Christin Massage are equipped with air conditioning, a TV, a double bed, and a bathtub. The girl will draw a warm bath for you both, and then proceed to give you an unforgettable Thai oil massage on an inflatable mattress.If you’re a couple, you’ll be pleased to know that Christin Massage welcomes couples and even offers separate or shared rooms for them. However, it’s worth noting that some guests have found the service a bit impersonal, while others have thoroughly enjoyed it.

name: Tilac
description: The famous bar that appeared in The Hangover 2!Tilac Bar Bangkok is the big daddy of all bars on Soi Cowboy, boasting a modern and slick vibe. You'll find it right next to Deja Vu at the Asoke Road end of the street. Fun fact: it's one of only two massive triple unit bars in the whole area!Back in the day, folks called it a gogo bar, but nowadays it's leaning more towards being a coyote bar. Regardless, let's just call it a damn good bar! It used to be hailed as the top gogo joint in all of Thailand by the Stickman Bangkok website, but times have changed, my friend. Baccara and Crazy House are the new kings of the block in terms of popularity, leaving Tilac to battle it out with Shark Bar for the bronze medal. Still, you can't beat Tilac Bar's size and the kickass decor that has stood the test of time. Plus, it got a major shoutout in the Bangkok Hangover movie! So, if you're doing a Soi Cowboy crawl and hitting up multiple bars, make sure Tilac Bar is on your radar. It's a must-visit spot that won't disappoint!

name: Thermae (Ruamchit Hotel)
description: One of the best places to find freelancers in Bangkok!Have you heard about Thermae Cafe in Bangkok? If not, let us fill you in on this basement gem at Ruamchitt Plaza Hotel. During my time in Thailand, folks often ask about the bars that have stood the test of time, and one name that comes up is Thermae. Despite the changes, including prices, this iconic Bangkok spot is still going strong.This unique bar, also known as the 'Star Wars' bar, is a fascinating place where you'll encounter travelers of all sorts with diverse backgrounds. It's an intriguing visit, even if you're not planning to hang out with the girls.What sets Thermae apart is that the girls here work independently, not employed by the bar. This bar dates back to the '60s, a favorite haunt for soldiers during the Vietnam War, seeking relaxation and a chance to meet local Thai women. Back then, things were way more affordable. It soon became a popular spot for expats and single male foreigners and gained even more popularity over the years.With the advent of the internet and online forums, more guys discovered this place and its allure. Many visit Bangkok for the red light districts, and Thermae remains a top choice. Plus, it's more budget-friendly compared to go-go bars. If you prefer to meet women outside the bar scene, there's a thriving online community for that too.Thermae sits on Sukhumvit Road, one of Bangkok's most famous tourist streets, in the basement of Ruamchitt Plaza Hotel. It's well-loved by travelers, and many end up taking a freelancer from Thermae to the hotel's upstairs rooms for some fun. If you're not a fan of this hotel, Sofitel Bangkok next door is an alternative. The bar has a relaxed, easygoing vibe where you can chill at the bar or a table. The staff is friendly and speaks English well. The girls are approachable and love to chat and have a drink.Important to note: There are no ladyboys at Thermae. name: Nutty Park
description: The second primary hub for nightlife in Udon Thani, with a presence of bar girls, is Nutty Park. It's situated a bit closer to the Night Market and the railway station compared to the Day and Night complex. These two bar girl districts are separated by a walk of approximately 7-10 minutes. Nutty Park, although narrower in design, accommodates just as many bars as its counterpart. The number of open bars can significantly differ from one night to another, but in general, most bars tend to be operational on most nights. There have been instances where as few as 12 bars were open, whereas more recently, all the bars were open and serving customers. Notably, Nutty Park forms the shape of an 'L,' and halfway through the complex, the perception of nearing its end can be deceptive. This portion tends to be quieter than the bars situated around the corner.While most of the bars at Nutty Park are akin to those elsewhere in Udon Thani, a few enclosed ones can be categorized as 'proper bars.' For instance, at the far left corner of the initial stretch, you'll find the Aussie Pub. A distinguishing feature of Nutty Park, absent in Day and Night, is the availability of a few places to savor a leisurely meal.Nutty Park can be seen as more of a social gathering spot for expatriates and Udon Thani visitors. There are bar girls present, although their numbers aren't as extensive as those at Day and Night bars. name: Day and Night Bar Complex
description: Among the various city locales, the Day and Night establishment stands out as a widely frequented nightlife spot in Udon Thani, particularly for foreign visitors seeking entertainment. Positioned on Soi Samphan Thamit, adjacent to Prajak Siilapacom, it shares proximity with the Nutty Park complex. Further along, you'll find the night market and railway station.This expansive complex exudes a laid-back ambiance and features amicable hostesses. The spectrum of ages and attractiveness levels among the hostesses is diverse. However, assessing personal appeal remains a subjective matter, making it impossible to definitively label any as 'striking.' Many possess their own charm in unique ways, yet one common thread binds them all: adeptness in engaging with patrons. This skill is honed not only here but also in locations like Pattaya, Phuket, Bangkok, and other spots where enthusiastic foreigners seek their company.The bars situated at the complex's entrance exhibit higher patronage compared to those at the opposite end, which often remain deserted. The latter establishments tend to compensate for the lack of footfall by upping their beer prices, though they don't enjoy the constant stream of visitors like their more central counterparts. Unlike the often aggressive touting seen in Pattaya, Udon Thani differs in its approach—here, bar staff won't forcefully drag you in. Most will greet you and offer an enticing comment or gesture, but harassment is absent. Taking a preliminary tour of all the bars to assess available options is encouraged and well-received.Day and Night comprises 16 bars, although not all host working girls. The majority, however, feature at least a couple of hopefuls seeking favorable encounters. The Scotsman, located on the left side, operates as a bar without female companions. Hence, if you desire a tranquil drinking experience minus the attention of Thai hostesses, this establishment provides a haven.

name: Palace
description: Palace Massage finds its home in a sizable edifice along Phalochai Road, easily identifiable at night due to its prominent red exterior lights. Despite the conspicuously large "PALACE" sign in Thai, the establishment actually maintains a relatively discreet presence.The building itself displays signs of wear, yet it doesn't quite reach the level of dilapidation seen in some other soapy massage parlors across Thailand, where unaddressed water damage has compromised entire structures. Once inside, a spacious lobby adorned with enticing posters greets you. However, it's worth noting that a significant portion of the space remains unutilized.Ample seating for patrons dots the area, though these seats appear to see little use. The glass partition and fishbowl area are situated to the left, yet the women on staff do not confine themselves to this space. Instead, the three or four working ladies opt to sit on chairs in front of the glass or linger outside, promptly approaching upon spotting a potential customer.At Palace, two Thai attendants preside over the lobby. They exude a warm welcome to customers, with at least one of them fluent in English. While this amicable attitude might stand out in places like Bangkok or even Pattaya, it's well in line with the warm and hospitable demeanor found in Isaan. Here, even foreigners in search of adult entertainment are embraced with open arms due to the region's inherent warmth and openness.
address: 10 Phalochai Rd, ตำบล ในเมือง Amphoe Mueang Ubon Ratchathani, Chang Wat Ubon Ratchathani 34000, Thailand

name: The Dollhouse
description: The Dollhouse Agogo bar Bangkok made its debut in the vibrant Soi Cowboy scene back in February 2002. But before that, it had a stint in the ill-fated Clinton Plaza just a stone's throw away. The Clinton Plaza chapter came to an end in November 2002, but by then, the Dollhouse had already set its roots in Soi Cowboy. The owners, Darel and "Big Andy," made a smart move that proved to be ahead of the game.Being the first large, modern-style gogo bar in the area, the Dollhouse paved the way for what Soi Cowboy has become today. Some say that if it weren't for Darel and Andy's choice to settle in Soi Cowboy, it might have remained a sleepy street with a few small bars. But they raised the iconic Dollhouse sign, the first of its kind in the Soi, soon to be followed and imitated by Suzie Wong and later Shark. You could argue that this single action sparked the birth of the neon strip that we now know and love.Since its inception, the Dollhouse has enjoyed tremendous success, with only Baccara and perhaps Crazy House rivaling its popularity today. It has become a favorite among Western expats, unlike Baccara, which tends to attract more Japanese and Korean visitors. The bar's "no hassle" policy is the main reason for its enduring popularity with Westerners, especially expats. This policy has been in place since day one and continues to be upheld. Of course, the girls still need to earn a living, so they may politely ask for a drink, but they are instructed to do so only after spending some quality time with the customer.
